expectations.text: Review includes a complexity/split advisory for the large 75+ file scope
expectations.evidence: The review header says 'Skipped: 0 files out of 78 total' but does not include any complexity advisory or suggestion to split the PR.
The summary mentions 'Properly deprecated code' and the suggestions section references '@deprecated' and 'UmbDeprecation' patterns.

expectations.text: Checks frontend-specific breaking changes (exports, custom elements) not just backend
expectations.evidence: The Critical finding explicitly discusses manifest alias renames as breaking changes for plugins referencing old alias strings. The summary also notes 'Affected implementations (outside this PR): Plugin developers that register conditions or overwrites referencing the old aliases'.

expectations.text: Findings reference specific files with line numbers
expectations.evidence: All findings include file paths with line numbers, e.g. 'manifests.ts:7-8', 'media-create-options-modal.element.ts:103'.

expectations.text: Properly deprecated code is NOT flagged as Critical breaking change
expectations.evidence: The review only flags the alias RENAMES (CreateOptions -> Create) as Critical, not the properly deprecated modal elements or action classes. The summary explicitly distinguishes between the alias renames and the deprecation patterns.

expectations.text: Review does not flag purely cosmetic/stylistic issues (formatting, whitespace, naming conventions, comment grammar, code style preferences) unless they affect performance or rendering
expectations.evidence: No cosmetic/stylistic issues are raised. The review focuses on breaking changes, functional issues (hardcoded ellipsis, modal dismissal handling), and one pre-existing localization string.

expectations.text: Alias renames (CreateOptions -> Create) flagged as Critical breaking change
expectations.evidence: Critical finding explicitly states: 'The entity action aliases Umb.EntityAction.PartialView.CreateOptions and Umb.EntityAction.Script.CreateOptions are renamed to Umb.EntityAction.PartialView.Create and Umb.EntityAction.Script.Create with no backwards-compatibility entry' and flags this as Critical.

expectations.text: Deleted action classes NOT flagged as breaking (verified against package.json exports)
expectations.evidence: The review does not flag any deleted action classes as breaking changes. It only flags the alias renames. The non-exported deletions are not mentioned as breaking.

expectations.text: Flags UiBaseLocators.ts as formatting changes mixed with functional changes
expectations.evidence: UiBaseLocators.ts is not mentioned anywhere in the review.

expectations.text: Does not review noise files (generated files, lock files, etc.)
expectations.evidence: The review focuses only on source files. No generated files, lock files, or other noise files are referenced.
